sections.label: Section 1167
sections.original: τῶν Λαΐου τοίνυν τις ἦν γεννημάτων .
sections.translation: It was a child , then , of the house of Laius .

sections.label: Section 1168
sections.original: ἦ δοῦλος ἢ κείνου τις ἐγγενὴς γεγώς ;
sections.translation: A slave ? Or one of his own clan ?

sections.label: Section 1169
sections.original: οἴμοι , πρὸς αὐτῷ γʼ εἰμὶ τῷ δεινῷ λέγειν .
sections.translation: Alas ! I am on the brink of speaking the dreaded words .

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: κἄγωγʼ ἀκούειν · ἀλλʼ ὅμως ἀκουστέον .
sections.translation: And I of hearing : I must hear nevertheless .

sections.label: Section 1171
sections.original: κείνου γέ τοι δὴ παῖς ἐκλῄζεθʼ · ἡ δʼ ἔσω
sections.translation: You must know then , that it was said to be his own child . But your lady within could say best how these matters lie .

sections.label: Section 1173
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ δίδωσιν ἥδε σοι ;
sections.translation: How ? Did she give it to you ?

sections.label: Section 1173a
sections.original: μάλιστʼ , ἄναξ .
sections.translation: Yes , my lord .

sections.label: Section 1174
sections.original: ὡς πρὸς τί χρείας ;
sections.translation: For what purpose ?

sections.label: Section 1174a
sections.original: ὡς ἀναλώσαιμί νιν .
sections.translation: That I should do away with it .

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: τεκοῦσα τλήμων ;
sections.translation: Her own child , the wretched woman ?

sections.label: Section 1175a
sections.original: θεσφάτων γʼ ὄκνῳ κακῶν .
sections.translation: Yes , from fear of the evil prophecies .

sections.label: Section 1176
sections.original: ποίων ;
sections.translation: What were they ?

sections.label: Section 1176a
sections.original: κτενεῖν νιν τοὺς τεκόντας ἦν λόγος .
sections.translation: The tale ran that he would slay his father .

sections.label: Section 1177
sections.original: πῶς δῆτʼ ἀφῆκας τῷ γέροντι τῷδε σύ ;
sections.translation: Why , then , did you give him to this old man ?

sections.label: Section 1178
sections.original: κατοικτίσας , ὦ δέσποθʼ , ὡς ἄλλην χθόνα
sections.translation: Out of pity , master , thinking that he would carry him to another land , from where he himself came . But he saved him for the direst woe .

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: κάκʼ εἰς μέγιστʼ ἔσωσεν . εἰ γὰρ οὗτος εἶ
sections.translation: For if you are what this man says , be certain that you were born ill-fated .

sections.label: Section 1182
sections.original: ἰοὺ ἰού · τὰ πάντʼ ἂν ἐξήκοι σαφῆ .
sections.translation: Oh , oh ! All brought to pass , all true . Light , may I now look on you for the last time — I who have been found to be accursed in birth ,

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: οὐ χρῆν ὁμιλῶν , οὕς τέ μʼ οὐκ ἔδει κτανών .
sections.translation: accursed in wedlock , accursed in the shedding of blood . He rushes into the palace .

sections.label: Section 1.1186
sections.original: ἰὼ γενεαὶ βροτῶν ,
sections.translation: Alas , generations of mortals , how mere a shadow I count your life ! Where , where is the mortal who

sections.label: Section 1.1190
sections.original: τᾶς εὐδαιμονίας φέρει
sections.translation: attains a happiness which is more than apparent and doomed to fall away to nothing ? Your fate warns me — yours , unhappy Oedipus — to call no

sections.label: Section 1.1195
sections.original: οὐδὲν μακαρίζω ·
sections.translation: earthly creature blessed .

sections.label: Section 1.1196
sections.original: ὅστις καθʼ ὑπερβολὰν
sections.translation: For he , O Zeus , shot his shaft with peerless skill , and won the prize of an all-prosperous fortune , having slain the maiden with crooked talons , who sang darkly .

sections.label: Section 1.1200
sections.original: χρησμῳδόν , θανάτων δʼ ἐμᾷ
sections.translation: He arose for our land like a tower against death . And from that time , Oedipus , you have been called our king , and have been honored supremely , holding power in great Thebes .

sections.label: Section 2.1204
sections.original: τανῦν δʼ ἀκούειν τίς ἀθλιώτερος ;
sections.translation: But now whose story is more grievous in men ’ s ears ?

sections.label: Section 2.1205
sections.original: τίς ἄταις ἀγρίαις , τίς ἐν πόνοις
sections.translation: Who is a more wretched slave to fierce plagues and troubles , with all his life reversed ? Alas , renowned Oedipus ! The same bounteous harbor was sufficient for you , both as child and as father , to make your nuptial couch in . Oh , how can the soil

sections.label: Section 2.1210
sections.original: πῶς ποτε πῶς ποθʼ αἱ πατρῷαί σʼ ἄλοκες φέρειν , τάλας ,
sections.translation: in which your father sowed , unhappy man , have endured you in silence for so long ?

sections.label: Section 2.1212
sections.original: ἐφηῦρέ σʼ ἄκονθʼ ὁ πάνθʼ ὁρῶν χρόνος ,
sections.translation: Time the all-seeing has found you out , against your will : he judges the monstrous marriage in which

sections.label: Section 2.1215
sections.original: τεκνοῦντα καὶ τεκνούμενον .
sections.translation: parent and child have long been one . Alas , child of Laius , would that I had never seen you . I wail as one who pours a dirge from his lips .

sections.label: Section 2.1220
sections.original: σέθεν
sections.translation: It was you who gave me new life , to speak directly , and through you darkness has fallen upon my eyes .

sections.label: Section 1223
sections.original: ὦ γῆς μέγιστα τῆσδʼ ἀεὶ τιμώμενοι ,
sections.translation: You who are most honored in this land , what deeds you will hear , what deeds you will behold , what burden of sorrow will be yours ,

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: ἀρεῖσθε πένθος , εἴπερ ἐγγενῶς ἔτι
sections.translation: if , true to your race , you still care for the house of Labdacus . For I think that neither the Ister nor the Phasis could wash this house clean , so many are the ills that it shrouds , or will soon bring to light , ills wrought not unwittingly , but on purpose .

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: ἑκόντα κοὐκ ἄκοντα . τῶν δὲ πημονῶν
sections.translation: And those griefs smart the most which are seen to be of our own choice .

sections.label: Section 1232
sections.original: λείπει μὲν οὐδʼ ἃ πρόσθεν εἴδομεν τὸ μὴ οὐ
sections.translation: Indeed the troubles which we knew before are far from being easy to bear . Besides them , what do you have to announce ?

sections.label: Section 1234
sections.original: ὁ μὲν τάχιστος τῶν λόγων εἰπεῖν τε καὶ
sections.translation: This is the shortest tale to tell and hear :

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: μαθεῖν , τέθνηκε θεῖον Ἰοκάστης κάρα .
sections.translation: our royal lady Iocasta is dead .

sections.label: Section 1236
sections.original: ὦ δυστάλαινα , πρὸς τίνος ποτʼ αἰτίας ;
sections.translation: Alas , wretched lady ! From what cause ?

sections.label: Section 1237
sections.original: αὐτὴ πρὸς αὑτῆς . τῶν δὲ πραχθέντων τὰ μὲν
sections.translation: By her own hand . You will not suffer the worst part of the painful event , since you do not behold the events . Nevertheless , so far as my memory serves ,

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: πεύσει τὰ κείνης ἀθλίας παθήματα .
sections.translation:
you will learn that unhappy woman ’ s fate .
When , frantic , she passed within the vestibule , she rushed straight towards her marriage couch , clutching her hair with the fingers of both hands . Once within the chamber ,

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: καλεῖ τὸν ἤδη Λάϊον πάλαι νεκρόν ,
sections.translation: she dashed the doors together behind her , then called on the name of Laius , long since a corpse , thinking of that son , born long ago , by whose hand the father was slain , leaving the mother to breed accursed offspring with his own child . And she bewailed the marriage in which , wretched woman , she had given birth to a twofold brood ,

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: ἐξ ἀνδρὸς ἄνδρα καὶ τέκνʼ ἐκ τέκνων τέκοι .
sections.translation: husband by husband , children by her child . And how she perished is more than I know . For with a shriek Oedipus burst in , and did not allow us to watch her woe until the end : on him , as he rushed around , our eyes were set .

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.original: φοιτᾷ γὰρ ἡμᾶς ἔγχος ἐξαιτῶν πορεῖν ,
sections.translation: To and fro he went , asking us to give him a sword , asking where he could find the wife who was no wife , but a mother whose womb had borne both him and his children . And in his frenzy a power greater than mortal man was his guide , for it was none of us mortals who were near .

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: δεινὸν δʼ ἀύσας ὡς ὑφηγητοῦ τινος
sections.translation: With a dread cry , as though someone beckoned him on , he sprang at the double doors , forced the bending bolts from the sockets , and rushed into the room . There we beheld the woman hanging by the neck in a twisted noose of swinging cords .

sections.label: Section 1265
sections.original: ὁ δʼ ὡς ὁρᾷ νιν , δεινὰ βρυχηθεὶς τάλας
sections.translation: And when he saw her , with a dread deep cry he released the halter by which she hung . And when the hapless woman was stretched out on the ground , then the sequel was horrible to see : for he tore from her raiment the golden brooches with which she had decorated herself ,

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.original: ἄρας ἔπαισεν ἄρθρα τῶν αὑτοῦ κύκλων ,
sections.translation: and lifting them struck his own eye-balls , uttering words like these : No longer will you behold such horrors as I was suffering and performing ! Long enough have you looked on those whom you ought never to have seen , having failed in the knowledge of those whom I yearned to know — henceforth you shall be dark !

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.original: τοιαῦτʼ ἐφυμνῶν πολλάκις τε κοὐχ ἅπαξ
sections.translation: With such a dire refrain , he struck his eyes with raised hand not once but often . At each blow the bloody eye-balls bedewed his beard , and sent forth not sluggish drops of gore , but all at once a dark shower of blood came down like hail .

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἀνδρὶ καὶ γυναικὶ συμμιγῆ κακά .
sections.translation: From the deeds of the two of them such ills have broken forth , not on one alone , but with mingled woe for man and wife . The old happiness of their ancestral fortune was once happiness indeed . But now today lamentation , ruin , death , shame ,

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: νῦν δʼ ἔσθʼ ὁ τλήμων ἐν τίνι σχολῇ κακοῦ ;
sections.translation: and every earthly ill that anyone could name are all theirs .

sections.label: Section 1286
sections.original: βοᾷ διοίγειν κλῇθρα καὶ δηλοῦν τινα
sections.translation: And does the sufferer have any respite from pain now ?

sections.label: Section 1287
sections.original: τοῖς πᾶσι Καδμείοισι τὸν πατροκτόνον ,
sections.translation: He cries for some one to unbar the gates and show to all the Cadmeans his father ’ s slayer , his mother ’ s — the words must not pass my lips —

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: ὡς ἐκ χθονὸς ῥίψων ἑαυτὸν οὐδʼ ἔτι
sections.translation: in order to banish himself from the land and not to put the house under his own curse by waiting here . And yet he lacks strength , and one to guide his steps , for the anguish is more than he can bear . He will soon show this to you : look , the bars of the gates are withdrawn ,

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: διοίγεται · θέαμα δʼ εἰσόψει τάχα
sections.translation: and soon you will behold a sight which even he who abhors it must pity .

sections.label: Section 1297
sections.original: ὦ δεινὸν ἰδεῖν πάθος ἀνθρώποις ,
sections.translation: O dread fate for men to see , O most dreadful of all that I have set my eyes on ! Unhappy one , what madness has come upon you ?

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: προσέβη μανία ; τίς ὁ πηδήσας
sections.translation: Who is the unearthly foe who , with a leap of more than mortal range has made your ill-starred life his prey ? Alas , alas , you hapless man ! I cannot even look on you , though there is much I desire to ask , much I desire to learn ,

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: πόλλʼ ἀνερέσθαι , πολλὰ πυθέσθαι ,
sections.translation: much that draws my wistful gaze : with such a shuddering do you fill me !

sections.label: Section 1308
sections.original: αἰαῖ αἰαῖ , δύστανος ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: Woe is me ! Alas , alas , wretched that I am ! Where , where am I carried in my misery ?

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: φθογγὰ διαπωτᾶται φοράδην ;
sections.translation: How is my voice swept abroad on the wings of the air ? Oh , my fate , how far you have sprung !

sections.label: Section 1312
sections.original: ἐς δεινὸν οὐδʼ ἀκουστὸν οὐδʼ ἐπόψιμον .
sections.translation: To a dread place , dire in men ’ s ears , dire in their sight .

sections.label: Section 1.1313
sections.original: ἰὼ σκότου
sections.translation: Oh horror of darkness that enfolds me , unspeakable visitant ,

sections.label: Section 1.1315
sections.original: ἀδάματόν τε καὶ δυσούριστον ὄν .
sections.translation: resistless , sped by a wind too favorable ! Oh , me ! and once again , Oh , me ! How my soul is pierced by the stab of these goads and by the memory of sorrows !

sections.label: Section 1.1319
sections.original: καὶ θαῦμά γʼ οὐδὲν ἐν τοσοῖσδε πήμασιν
sections.translation: No wonder that amidst these woes
